description: Archive of images uploaded for the Reflex Technologies site, held for later placement. uploads.html is generated from this manifest + the base64 data and is the durable, self-contained record (originals are preserved losslessly as base64 inside it).
masterOriginals: 2TB SSD (always connected) — the SSD is the master source of full-resolution originals; this source-images/ folder is the curated/processed working mirror of what has been uploaded.

images.title: Facility hallway — archival poster wall
images.caption: Interior hallway lined with framed archival posters, including the “We Can Save it! / Support Your Local Archivist” Rosie poster.

images.title: Color suite — full room
images.caption: The DaVinci Resolve color-grading suite: acoustically treated room, reference monitor, scopes, and grading console.

images.title: Color suite — grading console & scopes
images.caption: Blackmagic DaVinci Resolve panel and editor keyboard with waveform/vectorscope monitors and a film frame on the timeline.

images.title: Color suite — panel trackball detail
images.caption: Close-up of the DaVinci Resolve panel trackballs and lift/gamma/gain controls.

images.title: Machine room — deck timecode display
images.caption: Close-up of a video deck's timecode display and transport controls (REC / EDIT / PREROLL / STANDBY).

images.title: Machine room — Nagra 4.2 reel-to-reel
images.caption: Nagra 4.2 portable reel-to-reel audio recorder with tape threaded, on a stand.

images.title: Machine room — transfer rack bay
images.caption: The machine room: equipment racks (RK1–RK3) of Sony decks, reel-to-reel transports, monitors and patchbays for video & audio tape transfer.

images.title: Reception lobby — stitched panorama
images.caption: Panorama stitched from 3 frames: window signage and Barcelona chairs, the reflex Technologies logo wall, the entry door, and the test-pattern mural.

images.title: Reception lobby — panorama (retouched)
images.caption: Retouched version of the reception panorama: CoreGlow Pilates neon removed from the left window, ceiling AC vent removed and filled, then auto-leveled for contrast.

images.caption: Source frame 1 of the reception panorama: window signage, chairs, reflex logo wall, door.

images.caption: Source frame 2 of the reception panorama: reflex logo wall, door, edge of the test-pattern mural.

images.caption: Source frame 3 of the reception panorama: door and the full test-pattern mural.

images.title: reflex Technologies logo — stitched close-up
images.caption: Macro close-up of the dimensional reflex Technologies wall logo, stitched from two angled frames and cropped clean.

images.caption: Source frame 2 of the logo close-up panorama: '…eFlex'.

images.title: reflex Technologies logo — full angled shot
images.caption: Single-frame angled shot of the complete dimensional reflex Technologies wall logo.

images.title: 35mm Eastman color negative — hand inspection
images.caption: Gloved-hand close-up of a 35mm Eastman color negative strip (edge code EASTMAN 5217) held to the light for inspection.

images.title: 35mm film splicer (35TK) in use
images.caption: Gloved hands splicing 35mm film on a 35TK tape splicer with Quik Splice tape.

images.title: Loupe over a color film frame
images.caption: Film-inspection loupe magnifying a color frame, revealing the dye/grain structure.

images.title: Scanned film frame — portrait
images.caption: Sample scan: a film frame of a young person in a red patterned headscarf and turquoise beadwork, with the film-frame edges visible.

images.caption: Close-up of aged film wound in a rusted metal can, perforations and warping visible.

images.caption: Top view of an old warped film reel resting on a table.

images.title: Blue film leader — perforations macro

images.caption: Sepia-toned macro silhouette of a severely warped film roll.

images.caption: Stack of archival film boxes labeled NEGATIVE / DO NOT PROJECT on a counter in the vault, storage shelving behind.

images.title: Reflex film scanner — tension sensor rollers
images.caption: Close-up of the tension-sensor rollers on the Reflex film scanner with orange film threaded through the path.

images.title: Film prep room — wide view
images.caption: The Reflex film prep room: rewind bench with reels and rewinds, inspection lamps, Motion Picture Film posters, light table cart, and anti-fatigue mats.

images.caption: reflex Technologies 'Film Prep' room sign by the doorway, prep benches softly out of focus beyond.

images.caption: Gloved technician examining a strip of film with a loupe over an illuminated light box.

images.caption: Close-up of the Reflex scanner's illuminated logo and spinning reel (alternate frame).

images.title: Film scanning room — wide
images.caption: The scanning room: two Reflex film scanners with open transports and an operator at the color-grading workstation.

images.title: Film scanning — operator at workstation
images.caption: Operator at the scanning workstation, RGB waveform/scopes on screen, stacks of film cans alongside.

images.title: Reflex scanner — SmartGate 35 film path
images.caption: Open Reflex scanner transport showing the full film path, rollers, and the SmartGate 35 gate.

images.title: Film scanning — scopes & monitors
images.caption: Color scopes (waveform, parade, vectorscope) and preview monitors at the scanning station.

images.title: Reflex scanner — logo & reel (sharp)
images.caption: Sharp close-up of the Reflex scanner logo with reel and film threaded over the path.

images.caption: Operator viewing the open Reflex scanner (SmartGate 16) transport while film is loaded.

images.title: Reflex scanner — logo macro
images.caption: Macro of the illuminated 'reflex' logo on the scanner panel, shallow depth of field.

images.title: Reflex scanner — logo & reel 4
images.caption: Close-up of the Reflex scanner logo and reel with film threaded (alternate frame).

images.title: Reflex scanner — threading at SmartGate 16
images.caption: Gloved hand threading film through the SmartGate 16 gate and rollers on the Reflex scanner.

images.title: Still capture — transparency on the copy stand
images.caption: Hero close-up: a large-format color transparency (vintage street scene with flower sellers) held in the glass carrier on the still-image copy stand.

images.title: Still capture rig — copy stand (ColorChecker)
images.caption: Wide view of the still-image capture rig: macro copy stand, ColorChecker on the bed, dual light panels, reference monitor.

images.title: Still capture rig — copy stand (grid)
images.caption: Wide view of the still-image capture rig (alternate frame, grid overlay on monitor).

images.title: Still capture rig — overhead with ColorChecker
images.caption: Copy-stand rig from above: 55mm macro, ColorChecker Classic on the grid bed, calibration grid on the monitor.

images.title: Still capture rig — ColorChecker on bed
images.caption: Copy-stand bed with ColorChecker Classic, gloves and loupe, calibration readout on the monitor.

images.title: Still capture — ColorChecker under the macro lens
images.caption: Closer angled view of the ColorChecker Classic on the copy-stand bed beneath the 55mm macro lens.

curationPrinciple: Use only the very best images; close-ups/macros are preferred over wide shots. Picks: hero = site-ready standouts; keep = good detail backups; cut = wide shots, near-duplicates, and source frames.
